candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above financial items, but it is a total operating D&A line and is not specifically labelled as COGS/cost of sales. It sits separately from the selected COGS row, yet the excerpt does not show any bucket attribution between COGS and SG&A.
A more granular D&A component, but it appears beneath the broader 'Depreciation and amortization' row, so the summary row would be preferred if any D&A were selected.
Granular amortization component that appears to roll into the broader D&A presentation; also not specifically tied to COGS.
Looks like a balancing/detail line rather than a clean operating COGS D&A line; not suitable unless no better row exists.

variables.notes: No row explicitly identifies depreciation/amortization as belonging to COGS. The visible 'Depreciation and amortization' line is a separate operating total outside the selected COGS row, but the excerpt does not permit a reliable split between COGS-related and SG&A-related D&A. Conservative choice is to avoid adding anything to COGS_DA without manual review.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above financial items, presented separately from the selected XSGA component rows ('Selling/General exp', 'Employee benefit expenses', 'Other operating expenses'). If a separate add-back is needed, this is the best summary row.
Component of the broader D&A presentation. Should not be selected if the summary row is used.
Granular amortization line likely included within the broader D&A row; also not clearly an SG&A-only amount.
Balancing/detail line, not an appropriate primary operating SG&A D&A line.

variables.notes: The separate 'Depreciation and amortization' row is outside the selected XSGA component rows, so it could represent D&A to be added separately. However, it is a company-level operating D&A line and not clearly attributable only to SG&A/overhead; it may also include production-related D&A. To avoid misclassification and double counting, output is left empty pending manual review.
